entry.content: It seems SAP is moving to having users in the CIS user store for different activities.

entry.title: How to check database connectivity from os for java system?
entry.content: r3trans -d this for ABAP systems. What is command for Java system?

entry.title: Save the Date! SAP returns to ADIPEC 2024
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to announce that SAP will once again be participating in ADIPEC, one of the most significant global events for the Oil, Gas, and Energy industry. Taking place at the Abu Dhabi National Exhibition Center, UAE from November 4-7, 2024, ADIPEC will host key discussions and innovations that are set to influence the future of energy. and SAP is excited to be a part of these conversations.

Our theme this year, "The Power of AI," is especially relevant as artificial intelligence continues to reshape the energy landscape. SAP is committed to leveraging AI to drive advancements across the sector, and we’ll soon be sharing our focus topics that align with this theme.

Collaboration remains central to our approach, and we’re excited to work closely with our SAP Partners again this year. We’ll be announcing our full partner lineup in the coming weeks, so stay tuned for more details. 

SAP’s booth will be located in the Digitalization Zone, Hall 14, booth no. 14265. Our Solution Experts will be onsite to discuss SAP’s latest innovations and explore crucial topics such as the evolving advancements in AI, value creation in a decarbonized world and efficient operations and asset maintenance with the latest technology innovations and breakthroughs.

We encourage you to visit us at ADIPEC 2024 to learn more about how SAP can support your energy transition journey. Register here: https://www.adipec.com/visit/

For more information, please reach out to the following:

  • Daniella Maddock daniella.maddock@sap.com
  • Manas Sahoo manas.sahoo@sap.com

.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One Upgrade Challenges from SAP B1 8.81 to SAP B1 10.00 release family
entry.content: Just say no to this malarky, you HAVE TO do it with the prescribed intermediate version upgrade steps OR ELSE you lose the contractual SAP support of the damaged database. If you prefer to do it the right way, contact people who understand how to help you, for example https://propercode.co.uk

entry.title: Re: RFC_LOGON_FAILURE: SNC required for this connection for DDIC user in SUM
entry.content: https://me.sap.com/notes/3338929/E

entry.title: Re: ISD (input service distributor) GST complete setup in SAP ECC
entry.content: Hi Sumedh, could you please share if and how did you complete the setup in ECC?

entry.title: Re: RFC_LOGON_FAILURE: SNC required for this connection f...
entry.content:
3338929 - Central Note - Software Update Manager 2.0 SP19
https://me.sap.com/notes/3338929/E

go into this directory:    SUM/abap/bin/
create this file   SAPup_add.par   with this content: SNC_ENABLED = 0
repeat Phase

entry.title: how to fetch serial number in vl02n
entry.content:
hi experts.

In VL02N, you can assign a serial number in the extra —> serial number field. If someone writes a new serial number, it’s assigned to that material in that outbound. I want to fetch that and check it. How can I do that? I have tried many ways, but I can’t fetch that specific serial number under the save or PGI button for the first time.

it have to save for the first time then i can fetch it , but i want it before save for the first time.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud IAG - Issues and Resolutions
entry.content:
Dear Debashish,

I did recommended checks and are fine.

What is not working is the RepositorySync job providing following error message:

Error occurred during group sync: Service call return code : 422

SCI sysnc is working without errors.

Thanks.

Manuel

entry.title: Re: RRI jump option for the CDS Analytics Queries
entry.content: somehow I 've noticed that it is working on AFO but not in SAC.

entry.title: Re: How to create a value help for custom and released domain fixed values?
entry.content:
Hi @Andre_Fischer,

can this generic functionality also be somehow used as a text provider to display texts for keys of domain fixed values in a table? As the domain is currently only passed as part of the additional binding it would not be possible to create e.g. an association.

entry.title: Error:could not retrieve uom details for standard value
entry.content:
We need to send labor time back to ecc from SAP DMC after During Certain Calculation, by using create direct APi we are able to create the timing but while Approving the recorded time getting above error "could not retrieve uom details for standard value" with Message Code 500.

can you please help me on this.

 

Thanks,

entry.title: Payment terms returns to 0000 after pressing Enter in FB01
entry.content: I'm having issues in FB01. I tried to enter the payment terms 0101 but after pressing enter, it will return to 0000. How do I debug it?

entry.title: How to pass promp in SAC in case the source is Datasphere
entry.content:
Hey Guys 

I want to navigate from one story to another and also pass the variables but I cannot find a good solution for that.

I saw this blog post how to do it with normal model but this guide require me to get a model ID but I belive in case of Datasphere I do not have one.

https://cubis.be/sap-analytics-cloud-open-your-story-with-a-variable-constraint-via-a-url/

Do you know any alternative solution that works with datasphere ? 

Thanks Andris

entry.title: Re: WWI / chapter 14 / 3E Template
entry.content:
Hello @christoph_bergemann ,

It could just be, that the coding moves the "Authorization group" of the specification to the stack, to have something on the stack to indicate that a certain part/branch of the WWI coding has been reached. 

As the "Authorization group" is one of the very view mandatory fields in the specification DB, this value is used.

Hope that helps

Mark

entry.title: Clean Code - German Translation of "Clean SAPUI5" now available
entry.content:
German Translation of "Clean SAPUI5" 

We are thrilled to announce the release of the German translation of the Clean SAPUI5 book.

“Clean SAPUI5: Lesbarer und wartbarer SAPUI5-Code”!

Why This Book?

“Clean SAPUI5” is a must-read for anyone involved in SAPUI5 development who wants to learn more about clean code pratices and test automation. It provides invaluable insights and practical techniques for writing clean, readable, testable and maintainable code. Whether you’re a seasoned developer or just starting out, this book will elevate your coding practices to the next level. For leaders, an additional chapter offers insights on growing engineering skills and sustainable productivity within your team.

What’s Inside?

  • Good Practices: Learn the principles of clean code specifically tailored for developing SAPUI5 applications.
  • Real-World Examples: Understand concepts through practical examples.
  • Expert Insights: Benefit from the expertise of six seasoned SAPUI5 developers which aligned the practices with a large amount of software engineers at SAP.
  • Tips for implementing clean code and test automation practices in existing and new projects.

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to enhance your SAPUI5 skills. You can order the book on here or on other platforms.

Book Family

You can find more details on this book, the other books, the OpenSource style guide repository, the tools and related information in the blog Clean Code: Writing maintainable, readable and testable code.

Open Source Style Guide Repository

The original intention was to repeat the success of the ABAP Open Source style guide repository and make the content of the Clean SAPUI5 guide also available as OpenSource repository. So far there is only a InnerSource repository within SAP available. If you are working within SAP and are interested in maintaining the community for making the Clean SAPUI5 guide available the OpenSource style guide repository, please reach out to me.

Motivation

As authors, our primary goal is to provide a valuable resource for our community, combining insights from diverse, experienced engineers to improve SAPUI5 and ABAP development practices. Our main interest in writing the books in the clean code family are to provide a good reference for the community on how to write clean, readable, testable and maintainable ABAP and SAPUI5 / JavaScript code.

Thank you for being a valued member of our community and for spreading the word.

entry.title: Re: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2210 - Goods Issue - UOM Issue
entry.content: I had the same error, but not related with the language of the device. We got the error when user's language attribute in SU01 was set to "EN" (English) whereas the device language was "ES" (Spanish). Issue was solved when we adjusted the attribute to "ES". Hope this can be useful to you.

entry.title: Re: Risk analysis during request submission
entry.content: Hi Trilok, Did you find any solution for this problem ? I have similar case, Please share if you have any response, Best Regards,Hasan

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )
Re: Convergent Charging: Recurring Charge not triggered f...
entry.content: @Paul_Campbell The sentence "Activation process completed with status incomplete" means that some contracts cannot be activated. You have to check in the Rater logs files and trace file the reason of the activation failure. It could be a "no access" component in the rating logic or any other business error.

entry.title: Parser Error when changing SE16h outer join definition
entry.content:
Hi 

I'm using SE16H in ERP with SAP_BASIS 750 SP 25.

I have a working join condition with primary table VBAK and secondary table VBAP.

Now I want to add 2 more fields from VBAP, so I select KWMENG and VRKME:

But when I execute the selection I get an error:

If I create a new join definition with the same fields, there is no error, but every time I try to change an existing join, this happens.

Can anyone tell me what is happening here and how can I fix it?

Best Regards
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP typescript error "No call signatures"
entry.content: It finally worked with //@ts-expect-error. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio releases
entry.content:
Hi Manuelli,

Absolutely! There’s a place where you can find all the information about SAP Signavio releases and lifecycle updates. We regularly host release webcasts each quarter and hold product roadmap sessions twice a year.

Our most recent release was on August 6th, and you can watch the webcast on-demand here. For more details, you can visit our dedicated release page here on the SAP Signavio Topic Page within the SAP Community. The next release is scheduled for November 6th. Don’t forget to subscribe to our SAP Signavio Topic Page to get updates straight to your inbox.

If you’re interested in the roadmap, you can check out our latest session from the first half of the year here. We haven’t set a date for the next roadmap session yet, but we’ll post it on our Topic Page as soon as it’s available.

Feel free to reach out if you have any more questions or need further assistance!

Thanks, Samuel

entry.title: Re: RRI jump option for the CDS Analytics Queries
entry.content: I have seen that RRI functionality in SAC is only supported for BW systems and not for S/4HANA systems (explained in snote 2715030).

entry.title: Replace NULL with dash or blank in column with type 'Date' in SAC
entry.content:
Hi all,

Is there any way to convert null values of Date type Dimension with dash ('-') or blank (' ') in SAP Analytics Cloud?

I tried creating Calculated Dimension, but I've got an error: Dimension of type 'date' cannot be used in Calculated Dimension. Also, I've tried to convert date to string but function TOTEXT() is not allowed for Date type.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud IAG - Issues and Resolutions
entry.content:
Hi @Raffaella,

422 error in repo sync appears to be missing configuration issue with the integration. Have you checked and validated the necessary configuration steps?

Thanks,

Debashish

entry.title: BY USING RAP:FRAMEWORK
entry.content: IN RAP(SAP ABAP) Is there any possibility of develop a RAP API in SAP ABAP that handles both create or update the operation based on the incoming data. by user will just trigger create operation only?

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Munich 2024 (#sitMUC) - Secure Your Spot Now!
entry.content:
Munich has a thriving SAP Community Network – who knew?!

SAP Consultants, Developers, Architects and Project Managers meet regularly in their free time to share their knowledge, develop new skills, and network in a friendly, relaxed atmosphere. A wellspring of non-commercial groups are active here in our lovely international hometown. We may “fly under the radar” most of the time, but we’re always looking for ways to further develop ourselves and help each other along the way.

We’re proud to organize our own annual community-based event, SAP Inside Track Munich 2024, or fondly known as the “sitMUC”. Our motto “from the Community, for the Community” has guided us throughout the organisation of this event.

This time the sitMUC will be hosted in the brand new SAP TUM building in Garching. This year’s participants will be treated to an entire Saturday morning of informative and often entertaining presentations from community members and Mentors from SAP.
After a sponsored lunch, with time for chatting and getting to know each other better, we’ll move on to a new feature of the afternoon: an open “marketplace” of breakout sessions, where everyone is welcome to walk around, visit the various stands, stop and interact as you wish, or move on according to your interests. We think this type of atmosphere will allow for some very interesting networking and sharing of know-how and will help to spread new impulses within our community.

And here’s the best part: in keeping with our motto - “from the Community, for the Community” – the entire SITMUC event remains free of charge for all participants. Clearly, this would not be possible without the generous sponsorship of many individuals and firms within our community network. On behalf of everyone involved, we want to THANK YOU for your support, and for making our SITMUC the unique event that it is - simply not to be missed!

Our traditional pre-event offering is an official SAP CodeJam on the Friday afternoon before the SITMUC on Saturday. The topic for this year will be anounced soon. Due to limited space and high interest, the registration for the CodeJam and the SITMUC were coordinated, to allow SITMUC participants (especially those travelling from other cities) the first shot at a ticket. Needless to say, both events were booked out within a couple of days, and we’re maintaining an official waiting list for each. As with the SITMUC itself, the CodeJam is free of charge for all participants.

To those registered to attend either event, we want to say a hearty welcome in advance, and if you have any questions or comments in advance of the event, please feel free to contact us - we’re here to help.
Looking forward to seeing you here in lovely Munich on Friday, October 11th, for the SAP CodeJam and/or Saturday, October 12th, for the sitMUC 2024!

  • You can register for the event here.
  • Saturday 12 October 2024, from 09:00 to 18:00
  • Event location:  SAP Labs Munich (MAUE03)
  • Ageda will be published in a separate post in the coming days
    Stay updated with the latest information at SAP Munich Group / www.sitmuc.com/24

Cheers,

Your SITMUC Orga Team:
Björn Kuhlmann, Conrad Donau, Damir Majer, Gregor Wolf, Khaled El Ghali, Roman Becker

entry.title: Users active in BizX but to be made inactive in LMS - SF User Connector Issues & options
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Currently, we have the issue that not all users are LMS users, meaning that access to LMS is dynamic. (Field in BizX "No Learning" set to "X" determines if the user has access to Learning, via RBPs). Additionally, we are facing the challenge of having two different groups of users: Employee Central users who are also Employee Profile users, and users who only utilize Employee Profile. At the moment, we are unable to capture the Employee Profile users in the Integration Center.

For this reason, we have decided to create a report in the Report Center, which will then be sent to the SFTP server. The CPI will then transform it according to the following logic:

Status: active and NoLearning: X = New status should be inactive.

Status: active and NoLearning: 0 (or other number or letter) = New status should be active.

Status: active and NoLearning: Yes = New status should be active.

Status: inactive and NoLearning: X = New status should be inactive.

Status: inactive and NoLearning: 0 (or other number or letter) = New status should be inactive.

The output file with the modified values will be stored as a .txt file in the SFTP folder and should be picked up and processed by the LMS User Connector - SF

 

Questions:

Can this report be processed by the LMS Connector without any error messages?

If a user remains in an active status in BizX but is marked as inactive in the report because NoLearning is set to "X," will this user be processed in LMS? The status for this user should be set to inactive.

Info for you: As a contingency, we could configure it so that for all users with NoLearning = X, the email address is left blank. This way, the users would not receive any notifications from the system. However, it is crucial that the file is read and processed by the LMS Connector.


Thank you very much for your support!

Cheers,

Anna

SAP SuccessFactors Learning

entry.title: Missing graph labels and legend in webi
entry.content:
Dear all,

I have a Webi report that pulls data from a BW query that has 3 prompts.

I would like to add artificially a 4th prompt called "Enter XRate"

 

My idea was that when user opens the Webi, finds 4 prompts, fill them in and a variable stores the value entered in the 4th prompt.

Can I do this without creating a dummy query?

Thanks, best regards

Victor

entry.title: Re: undefined material in engineering replace with specific material in production
entry.content: Typical in SAP community - none of questions get answered. 20 year ago that was not the case - you ask question you get some answer of find somebody else question, which leads to solution. Now, SAP somehow made, that there is no answers

entry.title: Trigger Workflow upon Deleting Resignation Details from Job Information
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I hope this message finds you well.

We are considering a new requirement where HR admins should have the ability to cancel an approved resignation before the employee's Last Working Day (LWD). As part of this process, we need a workflow to be triggered upon deleting resignation details from Job Information.

Current Scenario:

  • Resignation details are initiated and recorded in Job Information.
  • We need to ensure that when HR admins delete the resignation transaction, a workflow is triggered.

Challenge:

  • Currently, in SuccessFactors, there is no functionality to set up a workflow for actions performed while deleting a transaction from Job Information.

Example:

  • If a resignation is recorded for an employee and HR Admin decides to delete this resignation transaction, the workflow should be triggered to handle this action accordingly.

Could you please check the feasibility of implementing this workflow trigger or suggest alternative solutions?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
It's not a https connection.
As i said, portal provides connection only with using of active'x components.
it's like crypto encryption, you cannot log in to portal without dongle.
The only way i see is somehow to force a program to start automaticly from user.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere Security & Data Access Controls Overview
entry.content:
This article and video are ideal for any system administrator or security architect to address common questions to ensure a successful implementation. Focusing on key items you should know sooner rather than later to avoid surprises. For example:

  • How user security works and the use of Scoped Roles with associated best practices
  • Why mapping on e-mail for SAML SSO could create users with inconsistent userIDs between SAP Analytics Cloud and Datasphere (it may not matter, but worth knowing why)
  • Why dynamic user creation when SAML SSO is mapped on e-mail could be problematic for Analytics Cloud, unlike Datasphere.
  • What are the benefits of a predictable userID in SAP Analytics Cloud unlike the same need in Datasphere.
  • How SAML SSO impacts Data Access Controls.
  • Overview of Data Access Controls and demonstrations of implementing each of the 3 structure options
  • How Spaces in Datasphere can be used with Data Access Controls to ensure users only see the data they should.
  • Finally, additional best practices

Feel free to post a comment, but if you'd like a reply please instead post a question. (Since only 'questions' enable replies and keep the context of the question)

 

Resources

 

Preview and presentation download (version 1.0.1)
Contents copied below into this blog post for easier access

Preview and download as Microsoft PowerPoint PPTx
Video - 1 hour 25 mins (version 1.0)

Video bookmarks

  • Application users
    • User access is granted by roles
    • What this means
    • Best Practice (likely to evolve over time)
    • Separate user directories (includes why dynamic user creation with SAML SSO mapped on e-mail could be problematic)
    • For SAP Analytics Cloud, there are benefits of a predictable USERID of your choice
    • Best Practice
  • Database Users
    • Demo of creating a new scoped role following best practice, assigning a user to a space, creating a view and consuming it as a database user
  • Overview row-level security
    • Concept
    • Structures with ‘Single Values’
      • Demo of creating Data Access Control with ‘Single Values’
    • Structures with ‘Operator and Values’
      • Demo of creating Data Access Control with ‘Operator and Values’
    • Structures with ‘Hierarchy’
      • Demo of creating Data Access Control with ‘Hierarchy’
  • Securing Data Access Controls with the use of ‘Spaces’
  • Additional practical and best practice advice

 

Application users

User access is granted by roles:

  • Global Roles:
    • Global roles grant privileges for Service (tenant) administration
    • The union of all global roles determines the privileges
    • Typically administrators need global roles, unlike regular users

  • Scoped Roles:
    • Grants both privilege and access to ‘Spaces’
    • Membership of the Space grants access to artefacts stored within, such as tables, views etc.
    • Users assigned to a particular space have access to all artefacts and data within that space
    • Access to a Space can only be granted via a ‘Scoped Role’, not a global role
    • Scoped Roles can not be set as ‘default’, which means new users are not automatically assigned a scoped role (see next slide for options)

 What this means

  • Granting access requires a single-scoped role since it grants both application privileges and access to Space(s)
  • The same user can have different rights in different Spaces
    • User could be a ‘viewer’ of one Space and a ‘modeller’ of another

Best Practice (likely to evolve over time)

  • Consider limiting re-using scoped roles for multiple spaces, especially if manually creating users and assigning them to Spaces
    • It isn’t always obvious when granting a scoped role to a user which Spaces they have access to
    • Use of SAML mapping for roles will add all spaces of the scoped role
    • The workflow to add roles to a user (Menu-Security-Users) will add all spaces of the scoped role
    • Adding a new Space to an existing scoped role, will add all existing users of that scoped role to the new Space
    • The following enables you to specify which Spaces of a scoped role are granted:
      • Command line interface (doc)
      • SCIM API (now available, however, SAP Provisioning Service support is planned later. Adding a role via SCIM adds all spaces of the scoped role)
      • Workflow to add users to a Space (Menu-Space Management)
      • Workflow to add users to roles (Menu-Security-Roles)

 

  • Avoid using standard application roles, instead use them as a template for custom global roles and scoped roles
    • Only the rights of template ‘Scoped role’ can be edited if those templates are ‘custom’ roles, not standard roles
    • SAP will update the standard application roles over time, which means you’re not in complete control when updates are applied. Always use a custom role instead.

 

Separate user directories

  • User accounts are held in each application, SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud
    • Each application has its own roles and authorization model
    • Means a user is created and maintained in each application

 

  • For both applications: When using SAML SSO and ‘dynamic user creation’ is enabled, the ‘user ID’ assigned depends on the SAML SSO attribute mapping:
  • When mapping on USERID, the USERID used
  • When mapping on email, the USERID is derived from the email address!
    • MATTHEW, would be the USERID for the email matthew@Sap.com

 

For SAP Analytics Cloud, there are benefits of a predictable USERID of your choice:

  • Users make sense of the SAC USERID, it appears within the user interface
  • Easier to control, row-level security (for SAC-acquired models) is based on SAC USERID
  • Gain insights easier as the activities log identifies the user by SAC USERID
  • Resolves sharing permissions when content moves about the landscape as some artefacts are stored within themselves who can access them by SAC USERID

For Datasphere

  • The ‘identifier’ used by Datasphere Data Access Control, is the attribute you map users upon
    • If mapping on ‘email’, Datasphere row-level access will be authorized based on ‘email’ unlike SAP Analytics Cloud which is always by SAC USERID

 

Best Practice

  • Map on USERID if possible (even when users may identify themselves by email)
    • Guaranteed to get a known USERID which is ideal for SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Can map on a different attribute for each application, but not great for consistency
  • The use of SCIM API resolves this problem regardless of the SAML SSO attribute mapping, i.e. even when email is used, the user ID can be fully controlled by you
    • Scimv2 is needed to resolve this problem, not possible with scimv1 (for both SAC and Datasphere)

Database users

 

Database only users

  • Users can also be created for ‘database’ only access
  • Such users can read and/or write based on database-level privileges
  • Each database user has an Open SQL schema automatically created for them
  • Ideal for 3rd party tools that require only view-level access

 

  • Such users can not enjoy Business Layer objects such as Analytical Models
  • SAP Analytics Cloud users must connect to SAP Datasphere as regular Application users (i.e. not database-only users)

 

Overview row-level security

Data Access Controls Concept

Application of row-level security requires:

  • 1) A ‘Data Access Control’ object (red outlined above)
    • Such objects contain a ‘Permissions Entity’ which is a table/view
    • 1 of 3 different table ‘structures’ must be specified
      • ‘single values’ (as shown)
      • ‘operator and values’
      • or, ‘hierarchy’
  • 2) Joining the ‘Data Access Control’ object to a table/view
    • The ‘permission entities’ are joined to the ‘data’ table thus reducing the rows accordingly – see 2 for ‘single values’

 

  • The data is then filtered by the user accessing the ‘data’ table
  • User identifier is always the same as the ‘Subject NameID’ used to map the user to Datasphere as part of the SAML Single-Sign-On
    • This can be a USERID, email, or a custom property
    • For the default Authentication method, i.e. without any custom SAML SSO, the identifier is the email
  • More details on ‘operator and values’ and ‘hierarchy’ structures next

 

Structures with ‘Single Values’

 Structures with ‘Single Values’

 

  • Row per value
    • For 100 values, you’d need 100 rows
  • Each entity is an ‘AND’ with others

 

  • Ideal for simple use cases
    • not necessarily the best choice for performance when lots of values are needed
    • Documentation

 

Structures with ‘Operator and Values’

Structures with ‘Operator and Values’:

  • Support much more complex logic
  • Requires a more complex ‘permission table’ to support operators, restriction, criterion, operator, first and optional second values
  • Support operators:
    • ALL (or *) - Provides access to all records.
    • N (or IS NULL) - Is null.
    • NN (or NOT NULL) - Is not null.
    • EQ (or = ) - Equal to First Value.
    • NE (or <> or !=) - Not equal to First Value.
    • GT (or >) - Greater than First Value.
    • GE (or >=) - Greater than or equal to First Value.
    • LT (or <) - Less than First Value.
    • LE (or <=) - Less than or equal to First Value.
    • CP (or LIKE) - Contains pattern First Value.
    • BT (or BETWEEN) - Between First Value and Second Value.
  • Documentation

 

Operator and Values

  • Based on these three records:
  • bob will have access to records with a:
    • (restriction 0) - Code between CA and CZ, and having a Type equal to 1,
    • or
    • (restriction 1) - Class beginning with ERR.
  • ann will have access to all records:
    • (restriction 0) thanks to the ALL operator.

 

  • The restriction defines the ‘OR’ and ‘AND’ operations:
    • Restrictions of the same value are AND’ed
    • Restrictions of different values are OR’ed

Based on these three records:

 

  • MSHAW will have access to records with a:
    • (restriction 0) - Code between CA and CZ, and having a Type equal to 1,
    • or
    • (restriction 1) - Class contains a pattern with ERR.

 

Structures with ‘Hierarchy’

Structures with ‘Hierarchy’:

  • The entity must have a semantic usage of ‘hierarchy’
  • Only external hierarchies with a single pair of parent-child columns are supported
  • Not supported:
    • Level-based hierarchies,
    • dimensions with internal hierarchies,
    • and entities with a semantic usage of Hierarchy with Directory
  • Hierarchy table must have:
    • A single root node with a parent value of null
    • No nodes with multiple parents
    • No circular relationships
  • Documentation

 

Hierarchy example

  • Based on these three records:
    • bob will have access to: Europe, France, or Paris
    • jim will have access to: France or Paris
    • ann will have access to: Paris

 

Securing Data Access Controls With the use of ‘Spaces'

 

Spaces enable Data Access Controls to be secured

  • ‘Permissions’ Space:
    • Only users with access can edit the permissions
  • ‘IT’ Space:
    • Data Access Controls can’t be defined on a shared table directly, so a view is needed
    • Users re-use the Permissions table (read-only) to define Data Access Control upon,
      …and define the joins with the data view, which is then shared
  • ‘Sales’ Space:
    • Users re-use the ‘Sales’ view (read-only) which makes it impossible to avoid the enforced Data Access Controls defined on the data
  • For more details see the official documentation

 

Additional practical and best practice advice

Consider the use of virtual tables for ‘Permissions’

  • Use virtual tables, instead of physical tables where necessary to reduce duplication
  • However, enable replication for source tables, particularly if any source contains more than 500,000 rows (doc)

Restrictions to restrictions!

  • Data Access Controls cannot be applied on views with input parameters
    • (limitation planned to be removed later)
  • View persistency cannot be performed on views protected by Data Access Controls with ‘Operator and Values’ structures
  • SQL Script within the view enables additional flexibility if needed:
    • Example blogs using hierarchies and wildcards, now redundant since they are supported natively

Re-using authorizations

  • Importing Remote Authorization from SAP BW and BW/4HANA is supported
    • (see blog post)

entry.title: Using NFC or similar contactless technology for labor tracking in DM
entry.content:
Dear DM Experts,

Is there a way to make it possible to do labor tracking in DM using NFC or similar technology? By default DM doesn't provide any possibility to do this. 

The requirement is that the worker scans his/her badge card on the shop floor to start the labor tracking automatically. For the scanning, the NFC technology is preferred instead of barcode scanning.

This was possible with SAP MII with custom development, however I do not find a similar solution in DM. 

Could this be possible with a custom POD-Plugin? 

Any ideas are welcome. 

Thank you.

Regards,
Oliver

entry.title: RAP (Rest full application programming) IN SAP ABAP
entry.content:
IN RAP is there any possibility of develop a RAP API in SAP ABAP that handles both create or update the operation based on the incoming data.

By user will just trigger create operation only.

Any Suggestions?

entry.title: Re: When importing CAMT.053 bank statement, error Bank Ac...
entry.content: Thanks - solution was to add bank key and bank account number into the Alternate Bank Acct ID Field. Now works fine.

entry.title: EBBR - Fixed Price Cost Based POC: Plan Cost is double what it should be
entry.content:
Hi experts, 

I have a billing WBS assigned to Revenue Recognition Key SPFC - Fixed Price, Cost Based POC. It's fairly straightforward, I have 1 work package assigned and there is 1 Purchase Order with 1 Line against this work package. I have received 1 invoice for the total amount of the PO. 

The planned costs on the work package are exactly the same as the invoice amount. When I open the app 'Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Projects' and I look at the billing WBS both the Actual Cost & Recognized COS are the same and equal to the PO and I don't have any planned COS. 

However, when I simulate the revaluation and go to 'Explain Simulation' it is giving me the correct actual costs, but the plan costs (could be capitalised, POC relevant) is exactly double my actual costs (and the planned costs in the work package). So for the Revenue Recognition calculation it's saying it can only recognize 50% of the revenue instead of the 100% it should take. 

However I cannot find these plan costs anywhere in the system. There is no accrual posting, I don't see any other plan costs against the work package or billing WBS. The project cost report app is showing me expected costs as well. So I don't understand why the calculation is suddenly doubling my plan cost amount. 

I couldn't find any SAP notes when I searched, so I hope maybe somewhere here has already had this happen before?

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Chart of Account renumbering after transactions in th...
entry.content:
Operating Chart of Accounts (YCOA):-

  • The operating chart of accounts contains the G/L accounts that you use for daily financial postings in your company codes.
  • This is applicable for all the company codes in S/4HANA Cloud system.
  • Assignment of YCOA- operating chart of account to company codes cannot be changed.
  • However, you can make small modifications to YCOA such as Description of Chart of                   Accounts, Maintenance Language (English, German etc.), and Length of G/L account                number such 6, 8, 10 etc.Kindly refer to the below SAP Blog for your reference: -
    Chart of Accounts in S/4 HANA Public Cloud (Cloud ... - SAP Community

entry.title: No PDF found
entry.content: Under investigation of "what's new 2408", I have met this error on preview screen of German VAT report(Umsatzsteuererklärung).

entry.title: Re: Assignment field in Group Reporting
entry.content:
Hello , In addition to the Assignment field, you can aggregate other fields to improve system performance and the consolidation engine, such as C/T Validations and IC Eliminations. There are many other fields you can aggregate, such as customer groups, vendors, products, etc. Please review the dimensions list. Based on our experience, you can aggregate up to 15 dimensions.

Check the our FAQ note.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "ABAP Core Data Services" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hi @Shilpa_Shankar / @sheenamk 
Greetings!
TASK 4 - Object Page is empty
To display the Objet page, I have added the UI.Identification annotation in the Metadata extension.
The code snipet is added below.
Issue: The Object page is empty even when the Identification annotation is added. As you can see the Header part is visible whereas the Identification part is invisible.

Requesting your guidance

 

@Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER
@UI.headerInfo: {
    typeName: 'Trip',
    typeNamePlural: 'Trips',
    title: {
        type: #STANDARD,
        value: 'Description'
    },
    description: {
        type: #STANDARD,
        value: 'TravelId'
    }
}

annotate entity ZV_S11_TRAVEL_BASE with
{
  @UI: {
      lineItem: [{ position: 01}],
      identification: [{position: 01}]
  }
  @UI.selectionField: [{ position: 01}]
  @EndUserText.label: 'Travel ID'
  TravelId;

  @EndUserText.label: 'Description'
  @UI: {
     lineItem: [{ position: 02}],
     identification: [{position: 02}]
  }
  Description;

  @EndUserText.label: 'Total Price'
  @UI: {
     lineItem: [{ position: 03}]
  }
  TotalPrice;

  @EndUserText.label: 'Currency'
  @UI: {
     lineItem: [{ position: 04}]
  }
  CurrencyCode;

  @EndUserText.label: 'Price with Currency'
  @UI: {
     lineItem: [{ position: 05}],
     identification: [{position: 05}]
  }
  PriceWithCurrency;

  @EndUserText.label: 'Status'
  @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{
     entity: {
         name: '/DMO/I_Overall_Status_VH_Text',
         element: 'OverallStatus'
     }
  }]
  @UI: {
     lineItem: [{ position: 06}]
  }
  Status;

  @EndUserText.label: 'Status Text'
  @UI: {
     lineItem: [{ position: 07}],
     identification: [{ position: 07 }]
  }
  StatusText;
}

 

Warm Regards
Surya L

entry.title: Exclusive for LAC: Enter the world of SAP consulting thanks to the "Join SAP Ecosystem" platform!
entry.content:
Dear Academic Community,

We are excited to announce the launch of our Join SAP Ecosystem – an innovative platform that offers various opportunities for those looking to enter the world of SAP consultancy.



💡This platform is the gateway to a career in SAP consulting. All individuals who register their CVs on Join SAP Ecosystem, whether beginners or experienced professionals, will be seen by our SAP Latin American and Caribbean (LAC) partners who are seeking talent!

Note: It's important to emphasize that this CV database is exclusive to new entrants in the SAP consulting market - established professionals will be excluded from the database. English skills are a plus, but not a requirement.

🫂 This platform offers unique opportunities to shape your career path in the areas of Consulting (Business), Project Management (PMO), and Development of Systems, all while gaining visibility with key industry players.

📢 Share this opportunity:

📧 1. Feel free to forward this information to your colleagues, professors, university students, or anyone you believe could have a successful career in SAP Consultancy!

🖇️ 2. For broader outreach, request our social media guide and share this amazing opportunity on your social networks (LinkedIn, Instagram, WhatsApp). If you choose to print any of the materials, please use recycled paper!

Got any questions? Please feel free to contact us at ignacio.gomi@sap.com 

Best regards,

SAP Future Talent Team

I just opened a case and there is no 64bit ECL Viewer yet.

"ECL viewer is a product from Siemens company. SAP has integrated this product in our SAP GUI transactions.

Currently Siemens is working on providing us a compatible solution for 64-bit of SAP GUI 800.

Official timelines of release for this product are not yet confirmed."

entry.title: Define Parameter Catalog for Subprocedure Determination in Sales Public Cloud

How can i add new field in Define Parameter Catalog for Subprocedure Determination in Sales for sap public cloud?

entry.title: Adding the discount more than once
entry.content:
Here's what they want to do: “Writing a custom action for promotions that gives a discount to the most expensive or cheapest product in the basket according to the selection of the business units, again in the amount selected by the business units.”

The code for this task is as follows.

private final String SELECT_OPERATOR = "selectOperator";

    @Override
    public void performAction(RuleActionContext context) {
        performActionInternal(context);
    }

    @Override
    protected boolean performActionInternal(RuleActionContext context) {
        CartRAO cart = context.getValue(CartRAO.class);
        Set<OrderEntryRAO> orderEntries = cart.getEntries();

        if(CollectionUtils.isEmpty(orderEntries)) return false;

        Task9Operator discountType = (Task9Operator) context.getParameter(SELECT_OPERATOR);
        BigDecimal discountAmount = (BigDecimal) context.getParameter("discountAmount");

        if (discountType.equals(Task9Operator.LOWEST)){
            applyLowestPriceDiscount(cart, orderEntries, discountAmount, context);
        } else if (discountType.equals(Task9Operator.HIGHEST)){
            applyHighestPriceDiscount(cart, orderEntries, discountAmount, context);
        }
        return true;
    }

    private void applyLowestPriceDiscount(CartRAO cart, Set<OrderEntryRAO> orderEntries, BigDecimal discountValue, RuleActionContext context){
        orderEntries.stream().min(Comparator.comparing(OrderEntryRAO::getTotalPrice)).ifPresent(orderEntryRAO -> applyDiscountToEntry(cart, orderEntryRAO, discountValue, context));
    }

    private void applyHighestPriceDiscount(CartRAO cart, Set<OrderEntryRAO> orderEntries, BigDecimal discountValue, RuleActionContext context){
        orderEntries.stream().max(Comparator.comparing(OrderEntryRAO::getTotalPrice)).ifPresent(orderEntryRAO -> applyDiscountToEntry(cart, orderEntryRAO, discountValue, context));
    }

    private void applyDiscountToEntry(CartRAO cart, OrderEntryRAO orderEntryRAO, BigDecimal discountValue, RuleActionContext context) {
        DiscountRAO discount = getRuleEngineCalculationService().addOrderEntryLevelDiscount(orderEntryRAO, true, discountValue);

        addDiscount(context, orderEntryRAO, orderEntryRAO.getQuantity(), discount);
    }

    protected void addDiscount(RuleActionContext context, OrderEntryRAO orderEntryRao, int quantity, DiscountRAO discount) {
        RuleEngineResultRAO result = context.getRuleEngineResultRao();
        result.getActions().add(discount);
        this.setRAOMetaData(context, discount);
        context.scheduleForUpdate(orderEntryRao, orderEntryRao.getOrder(), result);
        context.insertFacts(discount);
        context.insertFacts(discount.getConsumedEntries());
    }

But this code does not work exactly as intended. It makes a discount as a certain multiple of the entered discount amount (I still haven't figured out where this multiple rate comes from). According to my examination as a result of debug, the context structure containing the same information more than once comes with different references. Therefore, a discount with a new reference is created for each context reference and then added to the orderEntry as an action. Therefore, there is a discount more than desired.

Since I am a newcomer in Commerce Cloud, I have tried everything I know and I could not solve it. I wonder if there is someone among you who can help?

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba SNAP Overview
entry.content:
Hello Sujith, 

is SNAP compatible for integration with S4 Hana Public Cloud. could you please share your insights.

entry.title: Re: SAP Hybris promotion custom action bean error
entry.content:
I defined the wrong bean in the spring.xml file. I have solved the problem by looking carefully at the definitions in the official documentation.

<bean id="task9ProductNameRAOAction" parent="abstractRuleExecutableSupport"
          class="tr.nttdata.csa.csapromotionengineservices.rule.evaluation.actions.Task9ProductNameAction">
    </bean>

    <bean id="task9ProductAction" class="de.hybris.platform.ruledefinitions.actions.DefaultRuleExecutableAction">
        <property name="raoAction" ref="task9ProductNameRAOAction"/>
    </bean>

    <bean id="task9ProductNameRAOActionStrategiesMappingMergeDirective" depends-on="promotionActionStrategiesMapping" parent="mapMergeDirective" >
        <property name="key" value="task9ProductNameRAOAction"/>
        <property name="value" ref="orderEntryAdjustActionStrategy"/>
    </bean>

entry.title: Re: Dashboard content is not available
entry.content:
Hi @wiz_traceremo 

Please check 1969397 - Section 8 to solve your issue. 

KInd Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Omnichannel Sales Transfer and Audit a new sales transaction validation industry cloud solut
entry.content:
Hi, 

currently, we accept only sales transactions, financial transactions, totals transactions and control transactions. Apart from control transactions there are defined follow-on processes which can be triggered by the SOAP POS inbound interface of S/4. To my knowledge, there is nothing else that can be triggered through that interface. You can read more here in the documentation.
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/cde3584003724164b57ee9ab2398729d/729ad95981a54ef692eb3ba777e6eab6.html?locale=en-US&q=Retail%20POS%20inbound

for any further processing in S/4 you will need to take care of your own.

entry.content:

Object Page:

entry.title: Planning Segments in Monitor Material Coverage profile individual vs Net segment
entry.content:
When I search for difference between the Monitor Material coverage Net vs Net & Individual I am getting below. Link 

Monitor Material Coverage Net Segments:

  • Considers Planning Segments 20 and 22.

Monitor Material Coverage Net & Individual Segments:

  • Considers Planning Segments 20, 22 and 24.

They are saying these apps vary by planning segments consideration.

When I checked Planning segments I got this Link 

Net requirements planning segment : Ex Pln stg 10

Gross requirements planning segment :  Ex Pln stg 11

Storage location segment : When planning using MRP areas

Individual customer requirements planning : stg 20

Planning without final assembly : Ex Stg 50

1. Are these strategies or Requirement class OMPO?
2. If they are strategies which and all comes under 20, 22, 24?
3. which and all MRP elements considered un 24?

entry.title: Re: Using SAP CAP with IntelliJ
entry.content:
Exciting news: SAP CDS Language Support for IntelliJ is now available in the JetBrains Marketplace!
Just head over there and click the "Install to …" button to get started, or open the plugin settings in your IDE and search for "SAP CDS Language Support".

Note that the plugin is only compatible with the paid IntelliJ IDEs, such as IDEA Ultimate and WebStorm.

For the full readme, see https://github.com/cap-js/cds-intellij.

entry.title: Re: Planning Segments in Monitor Material Coverage profil...
entry.content:
Got the answer - SAP help 

Abbreviation Technical Key MRP Segment Description

NETPLA02

Net requirements segment

Consists of that part of the list for which the net requirements calculation in planning was carried out on plant level.

BRTPLA04

Gross requirements segment

The gross segment contains the MRP elements from gross requirements planning.

KNDPLA20

Individual customer segment

An individual customer segment is created per individual customer order for make-to-order production.

PROPLA22

Individual project segment

An individual project segment is created per project for project planning.

MSTPLA24

Segment for direct production or direct procurement

Segment is created for materials that are directly produced or procured without planning.

VORPLA30

Planning segment/ no final assembly

This segment groups together the MRP elements which are planned without final assembly, as yet. Once the sales order arrives the final assembly is initiated and is consumed with the planned requirements.

MSPLA32

Planning Segment for direct production or direct procurement

Segment is created for the planning of materials that are directly produced or procured.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content: Please find below my submission of week 4

entry.title: Involving Customer Feedback: New Outage Experience in SAP for Me
entry.content:
In today's 24/7 business environment, time is an organization's most valuable resource, especially when dealing with outages that require immediate resolution to keep systems and applications running. To streamline decision-making during such events and reduce solution time, SAP collaborated with four customers to develop a new Outage Experience within SAP for Me, the personalized central entry point providing quick access to SAP products, support, and services.

 

Actively involving customers

With the SAP Fiori Makers, SAP offers customer bootcamps to design solutions for specific use cases. Recently, adidas, Boehringer Ingelheim, Endress+Hauser, and BASF participated in a four-day bootcamp, gaining valuable insights into SAP Fiori and their end-user needs.

Customer Sascha Dörner, Head of SAP Cross Applications at Boehringer Ingelheim, highlights the success of these bootcamps and his user experience in a positive vein: “We were extremely surprised on how well the finished solution met our expectations and requirements. The methodology and implementation of the bootcamp deserves a special mention, as both played a key role in ensuring that the solution includes end-user feedback and brings real added value. I came to the workshop with a precise idea of the problem and how it could be solved. The professional approach opened further perspectives and dimensions for us, which ultimately led to an even better result.”

Supporting this view, Judith Schneider, UX Designer and SAP Fiori expert, describes the bootcamp setup and goals stating, “We include customers and users from the very beginning of our development journey. In doing so, new ideas flow and areas for improvement can be identified early on. Following this design-led development approach, our customers help shape the user experience according to their expectations.”

 

SAP’s design-led development process guarantees a solid and consistent user experience that addresses the needs of end-users across all lines of business, tasks, and devices.

To ensure that the outage solution will meet end-user needs, the main persona—an IT admin—has been defined and SAP’s Scenes tool has been used to visualize the user story during an outage. This collaborative process identified additional personas, key notifications, and communication channels, leading participants to create low-fi mockups and ultimately translate the use case into SAP Fiori for a high-fi mockup and a future software prototype.

With Scenes, storyboards about products and services can be created fast, collaboratively, and iteratively.

Benefits of the new Outage Experience in SAP for Me

The new Outage Experience in SAP for Me ensures that users stay informed with real-time event handling across mobile apps and smartwatches. Nathalie Pardini, SAP Product Owner, emphasizes, “This innovative solution gives customers the ability to customize outage notifications according to their specific business needs. By targeting specific users, businesses can ensure that the right people receive important outage information in a timely manner. Thanks to the valuable input from our representative customers, this solution has been widely embraced since its launch and continues to gain momentum.”

The new feature, Bundles, is a flexible grouping of cloud services and systems that offers three key benefits to SAP customers:

  • Customers can give intuitive names to Bundles and mark them with a business criticality, allowing for customized and prioritized notification management.
  • Notifications provide clear and accessible information about the specific parts of their business and processes affected by downtime, enabling customers to quickly understand the impact and take appropriate action.
  • Only relevant and impacted key users will be notified thanks to subscriptions on Bundles, ensuring that the right individuals receive timely and essential information. A notification history provides ongoing updates and post-incident summaries, complete with root cause analysis, helping prevent future outages.

Martin Buselmeier, Solution Architect Integration and Platform at Endress+Hauser, highlights the way this new solution meets their organization’s expectations: “Bundles are the perfect opportunity for us to group various SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP services that relate in various ways. By freely choosing the names, we can use Endress+Hauser's own terminology for recognition and quickly establish the context. Through the subscription, it is now also possible for us to automatically inform stakeholders outside SAP for Me, such as key users or project managers, about downtimes without having to involve an interpreter.”

The new Outage Experience now offers all the user-desired information to handle future outages most efficiently.

Optimizing products with user research – be part of it!

Engaging customers and end-users with their feedback early in the development of the Outage Experience was pivotal in creating a solution that genuinely met their needs. By gaining both a deep understanding of their pain points and needs, SAP successfully developed a product that resonated with users. As one end-user noted, "This is a very user-friendly and functional solution that truly helps customers adapt to specific scenarios or events."

This underscores the critical role user research plays in driving successful product development. In 2023, SAP introduced the SAP User Research Panel, a user research initiative that invites end-users to participate in remote research activities. They can share their product and design experiences, help shape SAP products to better align with their needs, while also offering them a preview of new features or products.


Make your voice heard! Join the SAP User Research Panel. Registration is unbinding and easy, taking part in research studies is convenient, taking ten to sixty minutes.
To learn more about the SAP Fiori Makers bootcamp offering, which includes a few days long physical workshop following the design-led development process, please visit their webpage.
Are you interested in more info about the Outage Experience and Bundles feature? Explore more in Nathalie’s most recent blog. For more information about SAP for Me, have a look here.

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content: Done

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI - How to create one summary log file and send...
entry.content: Hello Karthik, I would advise you to write log to data store.  Then you can create a separate flow to read logs from data store and email them to business owner. Data store will allow you to keep your logs even in the case if email delivery fails. You can trigger it or manually download the log from data store at a later time.

entry.title: Re: Comparison of SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office (SAC edition) vs. SAC add-in for Microsoft Excel
entry.content: @LavanyaJ Yes, Classic Account Model is still supported in AFO. As SAC recommends to use the New Model I didn't mention it explicitly anymore. Same counts for Datasphere Datasets.

entry.title: Re: Simplifying Data Navigation: Leveraging the SAP BW Report-Report Interface in SAP Analytics Clou
entry.content:
Help.

Does SAC support jump targets for Datasphere Analytic Models? Like the BW Live connections described above.

You want the ability to pass a filter from 1 SAC Story with a Datasphere Analytic Model to a 2nd Story/Datasphere Analytic Model. 

I have tried the hyperlinks with dimension filters, but this does not filter the target stories data set.

entry.title: Re: Error in BC set activation
entry.content: Please contact the SAP Support.

entry.title: How to count non-unique names from a list in SAP Story
entry.content:
How to create a calculated column in a database which returns "Unique" if user ID appears once & "Not Unique" if it appears more than once?

I have a database with a list of user IDs. Here, a user ID may be unique or may be repeated. Is there a way I can add a calculated column to my query which returns "Unique" for those user IDs that appear once in the database and "Not Unique" for those user IDs that appear two or more times?

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to run a program or background job only on individual PC?
entry.content:
Okay. Let's sum up. Please confirm if it's okay or not.

Currently, one user plugs a dongle on his/her laptop, then runs an ABAP program that you have created, which runs some ActiveX code via OLE (CREATE OBJECT, CALL METHOD, etc.) on the user's laptop, and it succeeds.

Now, what is your exact requirement? (and why "background")

entry.title: SAP IBP - RTI; Navigation to other Systems
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
in Order Based Planning / Real Time Integration it is possible to integrate several external systems in one planning area.

So that the system can distinguish the product ID, IBP adds the logical system to the product ID.

See:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_INTEGRATED_BUSINESS_PLANNING/68fa1e86fe6f41d98421d1ce13a08a9f/fcfae20979cb421183e092513d1a4128.html?locale=en-US

Example:
Prd-ID S4: FG126
Prd-ID IBP: FG126@COE

This link can be seen in the app: “Product Mappings - Real Time Integration”.

Now to my question:
I would like to jump into the S4 system via the Planner Workspace. There are also best practice instructions for configuring this.

When I implement these, the system jumps to the source system, but opens the selected app with the IBP product ID (FG126@COE) and not the product ID of the source system.

How can I change this so that the system selects directly with the correct product ID?

I have tried to change this with the help of an additional attribute in the planning area and by changing the source attribute in the Dynamic Attributes section in the Manage Navigation to Other Systems app. However, this means that the navigation option is no longer displayed in the Planner workspace. If I undo the change in PRDID, the navigation option is immediately available again.

Many thanks for your support

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "ABAP Core Data Services" developer challenge
entry.content: In your metadata extension file, annotate your CDS view with @ui.facet and specify the required properties. Facets organize information on an object page.

entry.title: Re: Domestic Revue GL should be Changed to Export Revenue...
entry.content:
Scenario 1 - Cancel original invoice
1) Once accounting effect is generated then SAP not allow us to
change so in that case we need to cancel original invoice.
2) After cancelling the invoice SAP generates a reverse
accounting effect of original invoice
3) Before creating a new invoice need to change in configuration part
as you know SAP not assign/define GL automatically against the
invoice some contra configuration happened in your case.
4) After change the configuration create new invoice

Scenario 2 - without cancel original invoice
If not possible to cancel invoice due to the legel or
any other reason in that case below is the option
1) Use FS00 t code and open GL account remove check box "Post automatically only"
2) Now you are free to enter journal voucher entry to settle the account
if account department okay
3) Again fill check box "Post automatically only" in FS00 t code

As per my knowledge Credit note is not a solution
if you generate a document with reference of the original inovice.

If you create a credit note without using a reference document in
that case you need to configure a credit note accounting properly.

Please inform if you require to change in configuration for invoice
or credit note.

Hope above solutions are works for you.

entry.title: Re: Domestic Revue GL should be Changed to Export Revenue GL after Buyon certificate submission in V
entry.content: Scenario 1 - Cancel original invoice 1) Once accounting effect is generated then SAP not allow us to change so in that case we need to cancel original invoice. 2) After cancelling the invoice SAP generates a reverse accounting effect of original invoice 3) Before creating a new invoice need to change in configuration part as you know SAP not assign/define GL automatically against the invoice some contra configuration happened in your case. 4) After change the configuration create new invoice Scenario 2 - without cancel original invoice If not possible to cancel invoice due to the legel or any other reason in that case below is the option 1) Use FS00 t code and open GL account remove check box "Post automatically only" 2) Now you are free to enter journal voucher entry to settle the account if account department okay 3) Again fill check box "Post automatically only" in FS00 t code As per my knowledge Credit note is not a solution if you generate a document with reference of the original inovice. If you create a credit note without using a reference document in that case you need to configure a credit note accounting properly. Please inform if you require to change in configuration for invoice or credit note. Hope above solutions are works for you.

entry.title: Re: Readiness Check Run for S/4HANA "FOUNDATION" System U...
entry.content: Thanks Greg for your help. This answers my queries.

entry.title: SAP MDG: How to make enable custom entity grey items in - Create Customer Search (HANA) ?
entry.content:
Hi 

Facing issue in choosing field grey check while create search view for Customer Search (HANA)

 

Thank You, 
Sowmya Surekha.
